fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Utah Dietitian License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a dietitian? Here's whether your license transfers to Utah - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: dietitian license reciprocity utah, how to transfer dietitian license to utah, dietitian compact utah, utah dietitian endorsement application

board.name: Utah Division of Occupational and Professional Licensing (DOPL)
board.physical_address:
160 East 300 South, 1st Floor
Salt Lake City, UT 84111

quick_facts.renewal_cycle: Every 2 years (expires Sept 30 of even-numbered years)
quick_facts.license_duration: Up to 2 Years (depending on issue date relative to Sept 30 even-year cycle)

license_types.title: Certified Dietitian (CD)
license_types.description: Utah's state-recognized credential to practice dietetics. It is commonly referred to as certification rather than licensure in Utah state law.

steps.title: Complete Required Education and Supervised Practice
steps.description: Complete a didactic program in dietetics and supervised practice accredited by the Accreditation Council for Education in Nutrition and Dietetics (ACEND).

steps.title: Pass the Registration Examination
steps.description: Successfully complete the Registration Examination for Dietitians administered by the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR).

steps.title: Apply for a Utah Dietitian License
steps.description: Submit an application for licensure to the Utah Division of Occupational and Professional Licensing (DOPL), including required documentation and fees.

documents.detail: Provide a copy of your current, active Commission on Dietetic Registration wallet card.
Completed application form with the $60 fee.

provisional_text: Utah does not offer a temporary or provisional certification for dietitians. You must possess the CDR Registered Dietitian credential before applying.
reciprocity_text: Utah offers licensure by endorsement. Out-of-state applicants with active CDR registration may apply by completing the standard application and submitting a copy of their active CDR card, which proves they meet the national standards required by Utah.

faqs.question: How much does a Utah dietitian certification cost?
faqs.answer: The initial cost is a $60 application fee.

faqs.question: When does the Utah certification need to be renewed?
faqs.answer: Utah Certified Dietitian credentials expire on September 30 of all even-numbered years. The renewal fee is $37.

faqs.question: What are the continuing education requirements for Utah?
faqs.answer: Utah accepts the continuing education completed for the maintenance of your national CDR registration (75 hours every five years) to satisfy state renewal requirements.

faqs.question: Does Utah have reciprocity for dietitians?
faqs.answer: Utah has enacted the Dietitian Compact, but compact privileges are not yet issuing. For now, dietitians still apply by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to Utah?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 2 to 4 Weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Utah while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: Most applicants need to wait for full approval before starting work because the state does not clearly offer a temporary or provisional credential.

reciprocity.notes: Utah offers licensure by endorsement. Out-of-state applicants with active CDR registration may apply by completing the standard application and submitting a copy of their active CDR card, which proves they meet the national standards required by Utah.

hero.title: Become a Dietitian in Utah
hero.dek: Utah Dietitian License requires completing an accredited dietetics program, passing the Registration Examination for Dietitians, and submitting an application to the Division of Occupational and Professional Licensing.
